dependencies.description: PIM acts on IGMP group membership to build/prune multicast trees. IGMP without PIM only provides local group state with no upstream forwarding.
dependencies.name: PIM

dependencies.description: L2 switches snoop IGMP to constrain multicast to only ports with interested receivers. Misconfigured snooping can block IGMP Reports from reaching the L3 router.
dependencies.name: IGMP Snooping (L2)

dependencies.description: On NX-OS, IGMP is automatically enabled when PIM is configured on an interface ('ip pim sparse-mode'). Without PIM, IGMP is not active.
dependencies.name: PIM on interface (NX-OS)

failure_modes.description: Hosts are sending IGMP Reports but the first-hop L3 router's 'show ip igmp groups' does not show the group.

resolutions.action: Verify PIM on interface: 'show ip pim interface' should list the SVI
Verify PIM on interface: 'show ip pim interface' should list the SVI
Verify PIM on interface: 'show ip pim interface' should list the SVI
Check IGMP version: 'show ip igmp interface <intf>' — match host IGMP version
Check IGMP version: 'show ip igmp interface <intf>' — match host IGMP version
Check IGMP version: 'show ip igmp interface <intf>' — match host IGMP version
Check join policy: 'show ip igmp policy statistics reports' for deny counters
Check join policy: 'show ip igmp policy statistics reports' for deny counters
Check join policy: 'show ip igmp policy statistics reports' for deny counters
On L2 switch: 'show ip igmp snooping mrouter' must show router port
On L2 switch: 'show ip igmp snooping mrouter' must show router port
On L2 switch: 'show ip igmp snooping mrouter' must show router port
Verify VLAN assignment and SVI state
Verify VLAN assignment and SVI state
Verify VLAN assignment and SVI state

root_causes.cause: PIM not enabled on the SVI/interface — IGMP is auto-enabled by PIM on NX-OS
IGMP join policy filtering the group — route-map or access-group denying the join
IGMP version mismatch — host sending v3 to a v2-only interface or vice versa
IGMP snooping on intermediate L2 switch not forwarding Reports to the router (no mrouter port)
Wrong VLAN — host in a different VLAN than the SVI where PIM is enabled

failure_modes.scenario: Hosts joining but group not appearing on router
symptoms: show ip igmp groups shows no entry for the group
Host application reports it has joined
show ip igmp snooping groups may show the join at L2
Multicast traffic not reaching the host

failure_modes.description: IGMP snooping is enabled but multicast traffic is not reaching host ports. Snooping is filtering traffic to ports that should be receiving it.

resolutions.action: Verify querier: 'show ip igmp snooping querier' — L3 PIM router should be querier
Verify querier: 'show ip igmp snooping querier' — L3 PIM router should be querier
Verify querier: 'show ip igmp snooping querier' — L3 PIM router should be querier
Check snooping stats: 'show ip igmp snooping statistics' for errors or report drops
Check snooping stats: 'show ip igmp snooping statistics' for errors or report drops
Check snooping stats: 'show ip igmp snooping statistics' for errors or report drops
Check lookup mode: 'show ip igmp snooping lookup-mode' — 'ip' is preferred over 'mac'
Check lookup mode: 'show ip igmp snooping lookup-mode' — 'ip' is preferred over 'mac'
Check lookup mode: 'show ip igmp snooping lookup-mode' — 'ip' is preferred over 'mac'
Verify group on port: 'show ip igmp snooping groups' should show host port
Verify group on port: 'show ip igmp snooping groups' should show host port
Verify group on port: 'show ip igmp snooping groups' should show host port
Check filter: 'show ip igmp snooping filter details'
Check filter: 'show ip igmp snooping filter details'
Check filter: 'show ip igmp snooping filter details'

root_causes.cause: No IGMP querier on the VLAN — snooping entries expire without periodic queries
IGMP snooping not learning the Report (e.g., received on a port not in the VLAN, or CRC errors)
IGMP snooping lookup mode mismatch (IP vs MAC) — hardware not matching correctly
Max-groups limit hit on a port — new joins are denied
IGMP snooping filter/access-group blocking the group

failure_modes.scenario: IGMP snooping preventing multicast forwarding at L2
symptoms: Host has joined (IGMP Report seen in capture)
show ip igmp snooping groups does not show the host's port for this group
Multicast traffic visible on uplink but not on host port
Disabling IGMP snooping on the VLAN 'fixes' the issue (but floods everything)

failure_modes.description: After all hosts leave a multicast group, traffic continues flowing for an extended period (minutes) before stopping. Wastes bandwidth and may cause issues for sensitive applications.

resolutions.action: Enable IGMP immediate-leave on the interface (only safe if ONE host per port): 'ip igmp immediate-leave group-list <ACL>'
Enable IGMP immediate-leave on the interface (only safe if ONE host per port): 'ip igmp immediate-leave group-list <ACL>'
Enable IGMP immediate-leave on the interface (only safe if ONE host per port): 'ip igmp immediate-leave group-list <ACL>'
Reduce Last-Member-Query-Interval: 'ip igmp last-member-query-interval <ms>'
Reduce Last-Member-Query-Interval: 'ip igmp last-member-query-interval <ms>'
Reduce Last-Member-Query-Interval: 'ip igmp last-member-query-interval <ms>'
Verify IGMP version — upgrade hosts to IGMPv2 or IGMPv3 for Leave support
Verify IGMP version — upgrade hosts to IGMPv2 or IGMPv3 for Leave support
Verify IGMP version — upgrade hosts to IGMPv2 or IGMPv3 for Leave support
Use IGMPv3 with explicit tracking for per-host leave tracking
Use IGMPv3 with explicit tracking for per-host leave tracking
Use IGMPv3 with explicit tracking for per-host leave tracking

root_causes.cause: IGMP fast-leave not enabled — router waits for Group Membership Interval to expire
IGMPv1 hosts on the subnet — IGMPv1 has no Leave message, relies entirely on timeout
Host not sending Leave message (application crashed, or IGMPv1/v3 report suppression)
Last-member-query-interval too high — slow Group-Specific Query cycle

failure_modes.scenario: Slow leave — group traffic continues after all receivers leave
symptoms: All hosts have left but show ip igmp groups still shows the group
Multicast traffic still flowing on the network after leave
Group eventually times out after ~260 seconds (Group Membership Interval)

failure_modes.description: No router is acting as IGMP querier on a VLAN. Without a querier, IGMP snooping group entries will age out and multicast traffic will either stop flowing to receivers or be flooded to all ports.

resolutions.action: Ensure SVI exists and is up with PIM enabled: 'interface Vlan<id>; ip pim sparse-mode'
Ensure SVI exists and is up with PIM enabled: 'interface Vlan<id>; ip pim sparse-mode'
Ensure SVI exists and is up with PIM enabled: 'interface Vlan<id>; ip pim sparse-mode'
If no L3 gateway on this VLAN, configure snooping querier: 'ip igmp snooping querier <ip>'
If no L3 gateway on this VLAN, configure snooping querier: 'ip igmp snooping querier <ip>'
If no L3 gateway on this VLAN, configure snooping querier: 'ip igmp snooping querier <ip>'
Verify with 'show ip igmp snooping querier' after fix
Verify with 'show ip igmp snooping querier' after fix
Verify with 'show ip igmp snooping querier' after fix

root_causes.cause: No L3 PIM router on this VLAN — PIM router is the natural querier
SVI is admin-down or does not exist for this VLAN
PIM not enabled on the SVI — IGMP querier requires PIM
IGMP snooping querier not configured as a fallback (when no L3 router exists)

symptoms: show ip igmp snooping querier shows no querier for the VLAN
IGMP snooping group entries aging out and disappearing
Intermittent multicast — works initially (unsolicited Reports) then stops (entries age out)
Multicast may start flooding to all ports after snooping entries expire

messages.description: Sent by querier to 224.0.0.1 to solicit group membership from all hosts on the subnet. Any host in any group must respond.
messages.name: Membership Query (General)
messages.when_sent: Periodically by the IGMP querier (every Query Interval)

messages.description: Sent to the specific group address to check if any hosts are still members. Triggered by receiving a Leave Group message.
messages.name: Membership Query (Group-Specific)
messages.when_sent: After receiving IGMP Leave for a group, sent Last-Member-Query-Count times

messages.description: Sent by host to the group address to indicate it wants to receive traffic for this group. Report suppression: if host hears another Report for the same group, it may suppress its own.
messages.name: Membership Report (IGMPv2)
messages.when_sent: In response to a Query, or unsolicited when host first joins a group

messages.description: Sent to 224.0.0.22 (IGMPv3 all-routers). Supports source filtering — host can specify INCLUDE (only these sources) or EXCLUDE (all sources except these).
key_fields: Group Record: Record Type (IS_IN, IS_EX, TO_IN, TO_EX, ALLOW, BLOCK)
messages.name: Membership Report (IGMPv3)
messages.when_sent: In response to a Query, or unsolicited when host joins/modifies group membership

messages.description: Sent by host to 224.0.0.2 (all-routers) when it wants to leave a group. Triggers Group-Specific Query from the querier.
messages.name: Leave Group (IGMPv2)
messages.when_sent: When host application closes its multicast socket or explicitly leaves the group

operates_at: Layer 3 (host-to-router signaling), Layer 2 (IGMP snooping on switches)
purpose: Internet Group Management Protocol — manages multicast group membership between hosts and their first-hop router. Hosts use IGMP to signal which multicast groups they want to receive.

states.description: No hosts on this interface have joined the multicast group. Router has no IGMP group state for this interface/group pair.
states.name: NO_MEMBERS

states.description: One or more hosts on this interface have joined the group via IGMP Report. Router maintains group state and PIM is notified of local interest.
states.name: MEMBERS_PRESENT

states.description: Router received an IGMP Leave and has sent a Group-Specific Query to check if other members exist on this interface. Waiting for responses before removing state.
states.name: CHECKING_MEMBERSHIP

states.description: This router is the IGMP querier on the subnet — responsible for sending periodic General Queries. Lowest IP address wins querier election.
states.name: QUERIER

states.description: Another router on this subnet has a lower IP address and is the IGMP querier. This router processes Reports but does not send Queries.
states.name: NON_QUERIER

timers.description: Interval between General Query messages sent by the querier to solicit group membership from all hosts
timers.name: Query Interval
timers.on_expiry: Querier sends a General Query to 224.0.0.1 (all hosts)

os_defaults.notes: Configure with 'ip igmp query-interval' under interface. NX-OS default is per RFC.
IOS-XE default is 60s, NOT 125s per RFC. Important cross-platform trap.

timers.tuning_advice: Lower values mean faster detection of group leaves but more query traffic. 60s is common for faster convergence.

timers.description: Maximum time a host can delay sending its IGMP Report in response to a General Query. Used for report suppression randomization.
timers.name: Query Max Response Time
timers.on_expiry: Host must have sent Report by this time or be considered non-member for next cleanup cycle

os_defaults.notes: Configure with 'ip igmp query-max-response-time' under interface

timers.tuning_advice: Lower = faster convergence but less report suppression benefit. 10s is standard.

timers.description: Interval between Group-Specific Queries sent after a Leave is received. Determines how fast a group is removed after last member leaves.
timers.name: Last-Member-Query-Interval
timers.on_expiry: Send next Group-Specific Query (up to Last-Member-Query-Count times)

os_defaults.notes: Configure with 'ip igmp last-member-query-interval' under interface

timers.tuning_advice: Lower = faster leave detection. Default 1s with count=2 means ~2s leave latency.

timers.description: Number of Group-Specific Queries sent after a Leave before removing group state
timers.name: Last-Member-Query-Count
timers.on_expiry: After last query with no response, group removed from IGMP table
timers.tuning_advice: Higher = more reliable leave detection but slower. Default 2 is correct for most environments.

timers.description: Time after which a group is removed if no Report is received. Calculated as (Robustness x Query Interval) + Max Response Time.
timers.name: Group Membership Interval
timers.on_expiry: Group state removed — router tells PIM no local members, PIM may prune
timers.tuning_advice: Do not set directly — tune Query Interval and Robustness instead.

timers.description: Time a non-querier waits before assuming the querier is down and taking over querier role
timers.name: Other-Querier-Present Interval
timers.on_expiry: This router transitions to QUERIER state and starts sending General Queries
timers.range: Derived: (Robustness x Query Interval) + 0.5 x Max Response Time
timers.tuning_advice: Derived automatically. If querier failover is too slow, reduce Query Interval.

transitions.trigger: IGMP Membership Report received from a host for this group on this interface
IGMP Leave Group received from last reporter (IGMPv2) or source filter change (IGMPv3)
Last-Member-Query-Count queries sent with no response — no remaining members
IGMP Report received during Group-Specific Query period — members still present
Group membership timer expires (no IGMP Report received within Query Interval x Robustness + Max Response Time)
Other-Querier-Present timer expires (querier went away) or this router has a lower IP address
Received a General Query from a router with a lower IP address

transport: IP protocol 2 (encapsulated directly in IP, TTL=1)

config_examples:
interface Vlan100
  ip pim sparse-mode
  ip igmp query-interval 125

notes.note: IOS-XE default IGMP query interval is 60 seconds, NOT 125 seconds (RFC default). This is a cross-platform trap when migrating from NX-OS. IGMP snooping enabled by default.

config_examples:
interface Vlan100
  ip pim sparse-mode
  ip igmp version 2
  ip igmp query-interval 60

notes.note: IGMPv3 is the default version on NX-OS. IGMP is automatically enabled by PIM — no separate 'feature igmp'. To change version: 'ip igmp version 2' under interface. IGMP snooping is enabled globally by default.

On vPC, IGMP groups should appear on BOTH peers. The 'ip igmp snooping mrouter vpc-peer-link' config controls multicast forwarding over the peer-link. This is a common source of issues in vPC multicast designs.
